C:no-history.statement: The spec never records how things changed, what was considered, or what was rejected. Git records history; the spec records intent.
C:no-history.provenance: "The spec is always current. A well-maintained spec is indistinguishable from one written in a single session."

C:no-evolutionary-language.statement: The spec never contains "Previously this was...", "Changed from X to Y", "Deprecated", or decision rationale prose. Declarative mutation only — rewrite in place, delete when removed.
C:no-evolutionary-language.provenance: "No tombstones, no deprecated markers. The feature is gone; the spec reflects that."

living-index.statement: The spec is a living requirements index maintained alongside the codebase; it answers what the system does and why at any point in time.
living-index.provenance: "The spec is a living requirements index maintained alongside the codebase."

rebuilding-test.statement: A fresh agent given only the spec and no prior context must be able to build a system that satisfies all user expectations; user-observable behavior would match.
rebuilding-test.provenance: "Could a fresh agent, given only the spec and no prior context, build a system that satisfies all user expectations?"

inclusion-test.statement: Four questions govern what belongs in the spec: (1) user explicitly stated → in spec; (2) competent developer default → skip; (3) choice between reasonable options → in spec; (4) derivable from existing entries → do not duplicate.
inclusion-test.provenance: "The inclusion test — ask these four questions in order"

property-graph.statement: The spec is a property graph (literally): node types Requirements, Constraints, Questions, Domains; edge types depends-on, implements, blocked-by, belongs-to; computable from plain text.
property-graph.provenance: "The spec is a property graph. Not metaphorically — literally."

single-hop-grep.statement: Single-hop queries (find entry by slug, all entries of a type, direct dependents, code implementing an entry) are covered by grep; no tooling required.
single-hop-grep.provenance: "Single-hop (grep covers these): Find entry by slug, find all entries of a type, find direct dependents, find what code implements an entry."

multi-hop-cli.statement: Multi-hop queries (blast radius, transitive dependency chains, domain coupling, blocked work, orphan detection) are covered by the CLI using an in-memory graph parsed from files.
multi-hop-cli.provenance: "Multi-hop (CLI covers these): Full blast radius of a change, transitive dependency chains, domain coupling analysis, blocked work analysis, orphan detection."

C:column-zero.statement: Every primary entry starts at column 0. Violating this breaks grep-based retrieval and the file parser.

C:two-space-indent.statement: Every sub-line is indented exactly 2 spaces. Violating this breaks grep-based retrieval and the file parser.

C:slug-unique.statement: Slugs are globally unique across the entire spec tree. Check with spec check-slug or grep before writing.
C:slug-unique.provenance: "Slugs are globally unique across the entire specs/ tree"

C:slug-permanent.statement: Once written and referenced in code, a slug does not change; the slug is a stable handle, not a description.
C:slug-permanent.provenance: "Slugs are permanent. Rename cost is high — choose carefully upfront."

requirement-node.statement: Requirement entries use bare slug at column 0, statement, optional ; sub-lines > provenance, ! assumption, ~ code-ref, @ depends-on, ? blocked-by.
requirement-node.provenance: "Requirements — observable behaviors the user expects. Use bare slug:."

constraint-node.statement: Constraint entries use C:slug: prefix at column 0, statement; sub-lines > provenance, optional ! and ~; no ; referenced from requirements via @ C:slug.
constraint-node.provenance: "Constraints — non-negotiable rules that govern all requirements in the domain. Use C:slug: prefix."

question-node.statement: Open-question entries use Q:slug: prefix at column 0, question statement; sub-line > what this blocks; deleted when resolved.
question-node.provenance: "Open Questions — unresolved decisions that block implementation. Use Q:slug: prefix. Always include a > line stating what they block."

provenance-required.statement: Every node has a > line (provenance). An entry with no > line is a ghost-scope error.
provenance-required.provenance: "> is required on every node. No exceptions. An entry with no > line is a ghost-scope error."

assumption-disclosure.statement: Whenever the agent fills a gap (number, threshold, choice, unspecified behavior), a ! line is required. "It seemed obvious" is not an excuse.
assumption-disclosure.provenance: "! is required whenever the agent fills a gap. Any detail the agent decided on the user's behalf gets a ! line."

code-ref-sublines.statement: Code refs use ~ on dedicated sub-lines (2-space indent), one per line; never inline in the entry statement.
code-ref-sublines.provenance: "~ moves to sub-lines. Never inline in the entry statement. Multiple code refs are now unambiguous."

planned-marker.statement: Unimplemented requirements carry until implemented; then add ~ refs, remove , confirm or remove ! lines in one edit.
planned-marker.provenance: "[planned] marks unimplemented requirements. When a requirement is implemented: add ~ ref sub-lines, remove [planned], confirm or remove ! lines. One edit."

slug-format.statement: Slugs are kebab-case, 2–4 words, describing the behavior not the domain. A developer reading only spec: slug in code should understand the constraint without loading the spec.
slug-format.provenance: "Format: kebab-case, 2–4 words, describing the behavior not the domain. The behavior test."

domain-file.statement: Line 2 of every domain file is a > one-line summary (domain identity); appears in spec orient; write so an agent can decide whether to load the file.
domain-file.provenance: "Line 2 of every file is a > one-line summary. This is the domain's identity string."

domain-split.statement: Split a domain into a folder when any of: more than 15 requirements in one file; a sub-domain has 5+ requirements; one section longer than rest combined; ## Refs lists 3+ distinct source subdirectories. After split: README.md as domain index, sub-domain .md files; slugs travel with content, zero reference updates.
domain-split.provenance: "Split thresholds — any one triggers a split. Slugs travel with content. IDs are never renumbered — slugs are the identity."

context-file.statement: CONTEXT.md contains project name, one-line summary, purpose, user goals, hard constraints, non-goals, domain map; no requirements; establishes context before loading any domain file.
context-file.provenance: "CONTEXT.md contains no requirements. It establishes the context every agent session needs before loading any domain file."

out-of-scope-section.statement: Each domain file has ## Out of scope as a short bullet list to kill incorrect assumptions before they form; constraint on imagination, not documentation.
out-of-scope-section.provenance: "## Out of scope — short bullet list. Its purpose is to kill incorrect assumptions before they form."

refs-section.statement: Each domain file has ## Refs listing primary code paths the domain owns; agents use it for impact analysis without scanning every inline ~.
refs-section.provenance: "## Refs — the primary code paths this domain owns. An agent doing impact analysis reads this section."

C:backend-readonly.statement: Query backends are read-only. They have no mutation methods; they never write to any file. Rebuild from files at any time.
C:backend-readonly.provenance: "SpecQueryBackend — reads from files or an index, answers read-only queries, never modifies files."

data-model.statement: The in-memory model includes Node (slug, type, domain, file, statement, planned, provenance, assumptions, code_refs, depends_on, blocked_by), Edge (from_slug, to_slug, type), BlastRadius, ValidationReport.
data-model.provenance: "The data model — Node, Edge, BlastRadius, ValidationReport as specified in the architecture."

backend-interface.statement: A SpecQueryBackend protocol defines read-only methods: get_node, get_all_nodes, get_nodes_by_type, get_nodes_by_domain, get_domains; get_edges_from, get_edges_to; get_slugs_for_path, get_paths_for_slug; dependents, dependencies, blast_radius; get_planned, get_open_questions, get_unconfirmed_assumptions, get_orphans, get_domain_coupling; find, check_slug_available; validate; rebuild, is_stale.
backend-interface.provenance: "Query backends are read-only. Any backend that implements this interface can power the CLI."

file-backend.statement: FileQueryBackend parses markdown files on every call; zero infrastructure, zero setup; deterministic; correct for scale up to ~200 requirements (under 100ms per call). Column-0 lines are node declarations; 2-space-indent lines are sub-lines with sigils.
file-backend.provenance: "FileQueryBackend (default, always available). Parses markdown files on every call. Zero infrastructure, zero setup."

json-cache-backend.statement: JsonCacheBackend wraps FileQueryBackend; serializes graph to .spec/graph.json and code index to .spec/code-index.json; checks file mtimes and transparently rebuilds when stale. Powers prov scope (O(1) code-index), prov impact (graph traversal), prov orient (domain summaries).
json-cache-backend.provenance: "JsonCacheBackend (phase 2, committed to repo). Serializes the parsed graph to .spec/graph.json and the code-path reverse index to .spec/code-index.json."

cache-committed.statement: The .spec/ cache files are committed to the repository. Their git diff in a PR is machine-readable record of spec changes; CI can fail fast on stale cache; fresh clone has up-to-date index without build step.
cache-committed.provenance: "The cache files are committed to the repository. A fresh clone already has an up-to-date index; no build step needed."

graphdb-backend.statement: GraphDbBackend implements the same read-only interface; syncs from files on rebuild(); optional external plugin for 1000+ requirements; swap via SPEC_BACKEND=graphdb; CLI and format unchanged.
graphdb-backend.provenance: "GraphDbBackend (phase 3, external plugin). Implements the same read-only interface. Optional; team at 50 requirements never needs it."

backend-selection.statement: Backend is chosen via SPEC_BACKEND env (file | json | graphdb), SPEC_DIR for path; get_backend() returns the appropriate implementation.
backend-selection.provenance: "Backend selection — get_backend() uses SPEC_BACKEND, SPEC_DIR; file, json, graphdb."

file-writer-separate.statement: Mutation is only through the file writer. Spec write writes to markdown files; query backends are invalidated after write (cache stale, graph DB incremental update if running). File writer enforces column-0, 2-space indent, pre-write validation (slug availability, dangling deps).
file-writer-separate.provenance: "When spec write creates or modifies an entry, it writes to the markdown file. It never writes to a query backend. SpecFileWriter is a separate concern from SpecQueryBackend."

rebuild-from-files.statement: Any backend can be deleted and rebuilt from the spec files; spec.py rebuild regenerates cache in seconds; no data at risk.
rebuild-from-files.provenance: "Any backend can be deleted and rebuilt. If the cache is stale, corrupt, or absent, python specs/spec.py rebuild regenerates it from the files in seconds."

C:cli-stdlib-only.statement: prov.py requires only Python 3.9+ standard library; no external dependencies.
C:cli-stdlib-only.provenance: "It requires only Python 3.9+ standard library. No external dependencies."

structured-output.statement: Every CLI command outputs structured plain text for direct inclusion in an agent's context window — formatted, labeled, actionable; not JSON or raw file content.
structured-output.provenance: "Every command outputs structured plain text designed for direct inclusion in an agent's context window. Not JSON. Not raw file content."

backend-agnostic-output.statement: CLI produces identical output regardless of which query backend (file, json, graphdb) is active.
backend-agnostic-output.provenance: "The CLI produces identical output regardless of which query backend is active."

spec-orient.statement: prov orient — start of maintain-mode session; output: === SPEC ORIENT ===, project/purpose from CONTEXT, hard constraints, DOMAINS table, OPEN QUESTIONS, UNCONFIRMED ASSUMPTIONS, PLANNED, DOMAIN COUPLING, next-step hint.
spec-orient.provenance: "Start of every maintain-mode session. Full product surface in a single structured package."

spec-scope.statement: prov scope <path> — before code work; input: file, directory, or file:line; output: every spec entry governing path (REQUIREMENTS, CONSTRAINTS, DEPENDED ON BY, OPEN QUESTIONS); or "No spec entries reference this path" with reconcile hint.
spec-scope.provenance: "An agent is about to read or modify code. Called before any code work. Compact; under 400 tokens."

spec-context.statement: prov context <slug> — full context for one entry; output: type, domain, implemented|planned, file path, statement, provenance, assumptions, code refs, constraints governing domain, depends on, depended on by, blocked by.
spec-context.provenance: "Agent needs to deeply understand a single entry — before implementing, modifying, or reasoning about its behavior."

spec-impact.statement: prov impact <slug> — before changing an entry or its code; output: direct dependents, transitive dependents, all code in blast radius, unconfirmed assumptions in path, planned entries in path.
spec-impact.provenance: "Before modifying, deleting, or significantly changing an entry or its code. Surfaces the full blast radius."

spec-find.statement: prov find <keywords> — free-text search when slug unknown; output: ranked matches (slug, domain, type, statement) or "No entries match" with orient hint.
spec-find.provenance: "Agent doesn't know the slug. Searching by concept or description."

spec-domain.statement: prov domain <name> — full domain load for editing; output: domain summary, file path, CONSTRAINTS, REQUIREMENTS (implemented/planned), OPEN QUESTIONS, OUT OF SCOPE.
spec-domain.provenance: "Maintain-mode agent loading a full domain to edit, review, or understand completely."

spec-validate.statement: prov validate — before every commit; non-zero exit on errors. Checks: dead-ref, phantom-slug, duplicate-slug, dependency-cycle, ghost-scope (ERROR); silent-impl, orphan-question, orphan-entry (WARN); unconfirmed-assumption (INFO). Output: ERRORS, WARNINGS, UNCONFIRMED ASSUMPTIONS, CLEAN, Result. Exit 0 only when zero errors.
spec-validate.provenance: "Before every commit. Non-zero exit code if any errors are found. Exit code 0 only when zero errors."

spec-diff.statement: prov diff [ref] — after writing/modifying specs; default ref HEAD. Output: semantic change manifest — IMPLEMENTED, NEW ENTRIES, MODIFIED, RESOLVED QUESTIONS, REMOVED ENTRIES, CONSTRAINTS CHANGED; assumptions requiring confirmation.
spec-diff.provenance: "After an agent writes or modifies specs. Produces the human review artifact."

spec-write.statement: prov write — structured input (JSON stdin or args): domain, entries (slug, type, statement, provenance, assumptions, planned, depends_on). Validates (slug available, deps exist, domain exists) then shows would-write block and Confirm; in autonomous mode agent writes and records in prov diff.
spec-write.provenance: "Agent is capturing new requirements from a user conversation or request. Takes structured input, validates before writing."

spec-reconcile.statement: prov reconcile <path> or --since <ref> — surfaces drift: phantom slugs (spec: in code, no entry), silent impl ( but code has spec:), dead refs (~ path not found). Output: sections for each drift type, CLEAN if none.
spec-reconcile.provenance: "Code has changed without the spec being updated. Surfaces drift between what the spec says and what the code contains."

spec-sync.statement: prov sync [path] — agent-facing drift report; detects phantom slugs, silent implementations, and dead refs; outputs structured plain text with resolution hints so the agent can discuss each item with the user and apply targeted fixes via patch sub-commands: mark-implemented <slug>, remove-ref <slug> <ref>, update-ref <slug> <old> <new>, remove-backlink <file> <line> <slug>; agent calls sub-commands after user confirms.
spec-sync.provenance: "Check the current spec and check the code. Understand drifts, ask clarifying questions to the user, and make sure we update the spec if required or update the code if required. All this should be handled by the agent."

spec-check-slug.statement: prov check-slug <slug> — before writing new entry; output: TAKEN with file and entry snippet plus suggestions, or Available.
spec-check-slug.provenance: "Before writing any new entry, to verify the slug is available."

rebuild-command.statement: spec rebuild (or equivalent) regenerates cache from spec files when stale, corrupt, or absent.
rebuild-command.provenance: "python prov/prov.py rebuild regenerates it from the files in seconds."

C:scope-before-code.statement: The agent always calls prov scope before touching code.
C:scope-before-code.provenance: "The agent always calls prov scope before touching code."

C:validate-before-commit.statement: The agent always calls prov validate before committing.
C:validate-before-commit.provenance: "The agent always calls prov validate before committing."

C:no-silent-gaps.statement: Every detail the agent decides on the user's behalf (number, threshold, choice) gets a ! line; "it seemed obvious" is not an excuse.
C:no-silent-gaps.provenance: "Never silently fill a gap. Every specific detail the agent decided on the user's behalf gets a ! line."

C:spec-code-same-commit.statement: One commit contains both the spec change and the code change; never split them.
C:spec-code-same-commit.provenance: "One commit contains both the spec change and the code change. Never split them."

C:no-direct-cache-write.statement: The agent never writes to .spec/ directly; the cache is machine-generated from the markdown files; CLI and pre-commit hook manage it.
C:no-direct-cache-write.provenance: "Never write to .spec/ directly. The .spec/ directory is machine-generated from the markdown files."

change-flow.statement: Every user request is handled in six ordered phases — understand (read spec and code), clarify (ask blocking questions, assume non-blocking with ! lines), propose (tell the user what will change and confirm before writing anything), write spec (edit domain files, validate, diff), implement (code with spec: backlinks), sync (prov sync, apply drift fixes, validate, diff, commit); phases must not be skipped or reordered.
change-flow.provenance: "Whenever a user asks for any new change, first we should be looking at this current spec, then looking at the references in the code if needed, and coming up with QA if necessary. First suggest an update to the spec, and the agent should tell how the system will change, what flows will change. Once the agent confirms the changes, the specs are updated and the code is updated subsequently."

spec-backlink.statement: Every function, class, or logical block that implements a spec entry carries a spec: comment at the top of the block; language-agnostic; one comment per block; multiple slugs comma-separated; slugs only, no description or file paths.
spec-backlink.provenance: "Every function, class, or logical block that implements a spec entry carries a spec: comment. Language-agnostic, always the same format."

coding-session-protocol.statement: Coding session (consume mode): (1) prov scope <file>, (2) prov context <slug> if needed, (3) prov impact <slug> before significant changes, (4) make code changes, (5) prov validate, (6) prov diff, (7) commit spec + code together.
coding-session-protocol.provenance: "Coding session (consume mode): prov scope, context, impact, make changes, validate, diff, commit together."

maintain-session-protocol.statement: Maintain session: (1) prov orient, (2) prov domain <name>, (3) write or update spec entries, (4) prov validate, (5) prov diff, (6) commit.
maintain-session-protocol.provenance: "Spec maintenance session (maintain mode): orient, domain, write/update, validate, diff, commit."

new-feature-session-protocol.statement: New feature session: (1) prov orient, (2) prov find <keywords>, (3) prov domain <affected-domain>, (4) clarify with user, (5) prov write, (6) prov validate, (7) prov diff, (8) implement code, (9) prov validate, (10) prov diff, (11) commit spec + code together.
new-feature-session-protocol.provenance: "New feature session: orient, find, domain, clarify, write, validate, diff, implement, validate, diff, commit."

debugging-session-protocol.statement: Debugging session: (1) prov scope <file-with-bug>, (2) prov context <relevant-slug>, (3) debug and fix, (4) prov validate, (5) commit if spec needed updating.
debugging-session-protocol.provenance: "Debugging session: scope, context, debug and fix, validate, commit if spec updated."

assumption-confirmation.statement: Human review of ! assumptions: confirm (remove !), correct (rewrite statement, remove !), or defer (keep !). prov diff is the primary surface; new ! always requires human review.
assumption-confirmation.provenance: "The assumption confirmation flow. When a human sees ! assumed X in a diff, they have three options: Confirm, Correct, Defer."

contradiction-surfacing.statement: Before touching spec or code, if the request contradicts an existing entry, stop and surface: Understood, Contradiction (slug + statement), Options (Replace / Add variant / Out-of-scope reject), Question.
contradiction-surfacing.provenance: "If the request contradicts an existing entry, stop and surface the contradiction with options."

pre-commit-hook.statement: Pre-commit hook: when specs/ (or spec/) files are staged, run prov validate (exit 1 on errors), then rebuild cache and git add .spec/.
pre-commit-hook.provenance: "If git diff --cached includes spec files, run prov validate; on success run rebuild and git add .spec/."

drift-reconciliation.statement: prov reconcile <path> or --since <ref> surfaces phantom slugs, silent implementation, dead refs; output includes resolution hints. After autonomous code changes, run reconcile, validate, diff and include in PR description.
drift-reconciliation.provenance: "Reconciliation handles the case where code diverged from spec. When an agent modifies code autonomously, run reconcile, validate, diff; include in PR description."

sync-session-protocol.statement: Sync session — agent-driven drift resolution: (1) prov sync <path> — read the full output; (2) present each drift item to the user with context; (3) ask the user what to do for each item (confirm intent before touching anything); (4) apply fixes via prov sync patch sub-commands or direct file edits; (5) prov validate; (6) prov diff; (7) commit spec + code together.
sync-session-protocol.provenance: "All drift resolution should be handled by the agent. The agent reads the sync report, explains each item to the user, asks clarifying questions, and applies the agreed fixes."

no-dead-ref.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when a ~ path in specs does not resolve to a real file or directory.
no-dead-ref.provenance: "Every ~ path in specs resolves to a real file or directory. dead-ref blocks commit."

no-phantom-slug.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when spec: slug in code has no matching entry in specs.
no-phantom-slug.provenance: "Every spec: in code has a matching entry in specs. phantom-slug blocks commit."

no-duplicate-slug.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when two entries share the same slug anywhere in the tree.
no-duplicate-slug.provenance: "All slugs are globally unique across the specs tree. duplicate-slug blocks commit."

no-cycle.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when a dependency chain forms a cycle.
no-cycle.provenance: "No dependency chain forms a cycle. dependency-cycle blocks commit."

no-ghost-scope.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when an entry has no > line.
no-ghost-scope.provenance: "Every node has a > line. ghost-scope blocks commit."

no-dangling-dep.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when an @ target does not exist as a slug in the specs tree.
no-dangling-dep.provenance: "Every @ target exists as a slug in the specs tree. no-dangling-dep blocks commit."

no-dangling-block.statement: prov validate reports ERROR when a ? Q:slug target does not exist as a question node.
no-dangling-block.provenance: "Every ? Q:slug target exists as a question node. no-dangling-block blocks commit."

validation-warnings.statement: prov validate reports WARN for silent-impl ( but code has spec:), orphan-question (Q: not referenced by any ?), orphan-entry (no ~ refs, no @ dependents, not planned); warnings do not fail the build.
validation-warnings.provenance: "Warnings: silent-impl, orphan-question, orphan-entry. Warnings do not fail the build."
